teams.name: Alabama

coach.name: Nate Oats
coach.notes: Best coaching job at Alabama per analysts

key_players.name: Aiden Sherrell
key_players.stats: Notable 8-block game vs Clemson, 5 pts, 10 reb

key_players.name: Taylor Bol Bowen
key_players.notes: Florida State transfer, dealing with numerous injuries all season

injuries: Davion Hannah — OUT for rest of year
Keitenn Bristow — OUT for rest of year
Collins Onyejiaka — OUT (heart condition), not cleared for full workouts
Taylor Bol Bowen — injury concerns, health for March critical
strengths: Elite offense — 92.9 PPG (1st nationally), 122.5 AdjO (11th)
Strength of schedule rating 14.16 (2nd nationally)
Won 6 straight and 9-1 stretch to close regular season
Finished 2nd in SEC
weaknesses: Worst defense among top teams — 83.6 PPG allowed (355th nationally)
Defensive rating 110.2 (277th nationally)
Multiple key injuries — Oats calls it 'most random unlucky injuries' ever
Defensive inconsistency makes them volatile in tournament

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Any team with strong halfcourt offense can exploit poor defense
upset_factors.three_point_variance: High — offense relies on volume shooting
upset_factors.notes: Floor/ceiling team: could lose Round 1 or reach Elite 8

teams.name: Arkansas

kenpom.notes: Dynamic scoring (6th AdjO), unstable stops (44th AdjD)

coach.name: John Calipari
coach.tournament_experience: National Champion (2012 Kentucky), 4 Final Fours, 900+ career wins (5th all-time)
coach.notes: Reached 900th Division I win in 2026 regular season finale vs Missouri

key_players.name: Darius Acuff Jr.
key_players.stats: SEC Player of the Year, Freshman of the Year, First Team All-SEC
key_players.notes: Projected top-10 NBA Draft pick, one-and-done expected

strengths: SEC Player of the Year (Acuff) — franchise player
Calipari's tournament pedigree (4 Final Fours, 1 title)
Dynamic scoring ability with top-6 offense nationally
2025 tournament experience (Sweet Sixteen run as 10 seed)
weaknesses: Defensive inconsistency — unstable stops
Heavy reliance on Acuff — supporting cast needs to step up
Calipari Year 2 at Arkansas still building culture

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Can be beaten by disciplined defensive teams
upset_factors.notes: Calipari has historically underperformed seeds in tournament — but also has upset DNA

teams.name: Kansas

coach.name: Bill Self
coach.tournament_experience: 2 National Championships (2008, 2022), 4 Final Fours, all-time great tournament coach
coach.notes: Managing Darryn Peterson's health and availability has been season storyline

key_players.name: Darryn Peterson
key_players.notes: No. 1 overall 2025 recruit, projected No. 1 pick in 2026 NBA Draft. Missed 11 games + multiple midgame exits due to cramping. 27.2 MPG avg.

key_players.name: Tre White
key_players.stats: 23 pts in key game, 6-of-9 FG, 3-of-4 from 3
key_players.notes: Picks up slack when Peterson is out

injuries: Darryn Peterson — recurring cramping issues, midgame exits throughout season. Bill Self: 'It's a concern'
strengths: Elite defense — 10th in AdjD nationally
Darryn Peterson generational talent when healthy
Bill Self's tournament coaching pedigree
Finished 3rd in Big 12 (behind Arizona and Houston)
Beat Kansas State 104-85 in senior night blowout
weaknesses: Peterson health is massive question mark — missed 11 games
Offense middling (53rd AdjO) when Peterson is limited
Inconsistency due to lineup disruptions
Self admitted he's unsure if he's truly coached Peterson through sustained stretch

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: If Peterson goes down or has cramping, offense can stall
upset_factors.notes: Seth Greenberg says Kansas is THE team to watch in March. Upside of a 1-seed, downside of a second round exit.

teams.name: Tennessee

kenpom.notes: Elite defense (15th AdjD), solid offense (36th AdjO). Balanced profile.

coach.name: Rick Barnes
coach.tournament_experience: 7 consecutive NCAA appearances (2018-2025), 9th on all-time D1 wins list, 836 career wins. 2 Elite Eights in last 2 years.
coach.notes: First among active coaches in career wins

key_players.name: Bishop Boswell
key_players.notes: Returning player, praised for offseason development

strengths: Elite defense — 68.9 PPG allowed (55th nationally), AdjD rank 15
Strong SRS: 22.44 (17th nationally)
Excellent coaching pedigree — Barnes has 2 Elite Eights in last 2 years
Versatile roster built on defense and upside
Strong transition game — 16-4 fast-break points edge vs Louisville
weaknesses: Shooting guard position light on proven production
10 losses — most among this tier
Newcomer integration questions
Not as explosive offensively as other SEC teams

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Teams with elite shooting can stress their offense
upset_factors.notes: Rick Barnes' tournament track record is strong — back-to-back Elite Eights. Defense-first identity travels well in March.

teams.name: Saint John's

kenpom.notes: Elite defense (14th AdjD), offense can stall (51st AdjO). Controls tempo and suppresses efficiency.

coach.name: Rick Pitino
coach.tournament_experience: 2 National Championships (1996 Kentucky, 2013 Louisville), multiple Final Fours. Hall of Famer.
coach.notes: Led St. John's to first outright regular season title since 1985. No. 1 ranked transfer class.

key_players.name: Zuby Ejiofor
key_players.stats: Big East Preseason Player of the Year
key_players.notes: 6-9 senior, one of only 2 returnees from 2023-24 roster

key_players.name: Ian Jackson
key_players.notes: 6-5 guard, UNC transfer, second-leading scorer as freshman

key_players.name: Dylan Darling
key_players.stats: Big Sky Player of the Year at Idaho State
key_players.notes: 6-1, only true point guard on roster

strengths: Elite defense — 14th nationally in AdjD
Rick Pitino's tournament pedigree — 2 national titles
Depth of shooting: Jackson (40%), Sellers (40%), Sanon (37%)
Won Big East regular season and tournament last year (31-5)
Top-ranked transfer class built for March
weaknesses: Offense can stall — 51st in AdjO
Only 2 returnees from prior roster — chemistry concerns
Heavy reliance on transfers finding cohesion
Lost to 10-seed Arkansas in 2025 Round of 32 — early exit baggage

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Teams that can break their tempo and force up-and-down pace
upset_factors.three_point_variance: Multiple 37-40% shooters — consistent but not explosive
upset_factors.notes: Legitimate Final Four contender. Pitino's teams historically peak in March. Defense-first identity travels well.

teams.name: Saint Mary's

kenpom.notes: Strong defense (17th AdjD), solid offense (43rd AdjO). High AdjEM due to elite defense.

coach.name: Randy Bennett
coach.tournament_experience: Multiple NCAA appearances, consistent WCC contender alongside Gonzaga

strengths: Elite defense — 64.5 PPG allowed (12th nationally), AdjD rank 17
Strong rebounding (McKeever 9.5 RPG)
Disciplined, well-coached program under Bennett
26-5 record with dominant WCC performance (15-1)
weaknesses: Weak strength of schedule — SOS 4.01 (87th), WCC not a power conference
Leading scorer Lewis shooting only 38.6% from field
May struggle against elite athletic teams from power conferences
Limited marquee wins to prove tournament readiness
teams.conference_tournament_status: Entering WCC Tournament as top seed

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Athletic, fast-paced teams can overwhelm deliberate style
upset_factors.notes: Classic mid-major that can grind out wins. Randy Bennett teams historically play well in first-round upsets but struggle in later rounds.

teams.name: North Carolina

kenpom.notes: Balanced profile — 35th offense, 34th defense. No elite strength.

coach.name: Hubert Davis
coach.tournament_experience: National Championship Game (2022), multiple NCAA appearances
coach.notes: Lost virtually entire core from 2024-25 — massive roster turnover

key_players.name: Seth Trimble
key_players.notes: Only main rotation player who returned from 2024-25

strengths: 24-7 record despite massive roster turnover — impressive coaching
Blue-blood program with championship pedigree
Balanced offensive and defensive profile
Strong non-conference schedule with Kansas and Kentucky
weaknesses: Lost all 5 starters from 2024-25 (RJ Davis, Withers, Cadeau, Jackson, Washington, Lubin all gone)
KenPom rank (30th) lower than NET rank (23rd) — may be slightly overrated
No proven go-to scorer in crunch time
2025 tournament: early exit as 11 seed (lost to Ole Miss in Round of 64)

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Teams with dominant individual scorers can exploit lack of go-to player
upset_factors.notes: Blue-blood deflator applies. UNC historically gets favorable treatment in seeding. Hubert Davis has mixed March results.

teams.name: BYU

kenpom.notes: Offensive powerhouse (11th AdjO), defensive liability (68th AdjD). Similar profile to Alabama.

coach.name: Kevin Young
coach.tournament_experience: 2025 Sweet Sixteen in first year as HC
coach.notes: Former NBA assistant, building program quickly

strengths: Elite offense — 84.4 PPG, 11th in AdjO nationally
Big 12 conference battle-tested (though 8-9 in league)
Made Sweet Sixteen in 2025 (beat VCU and Wisconsin)
Kevin Young's NBA coaching background
weaknesses: Poor defense — 75.8 PPG allowed, 68th in AdjD
Below .500 in Big 12 play (8-9)
Got blown out by Alabama 113-88 in 2025 Sweet Sixteen
10 losses suggest inconsistency

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Elite offensive teams can outscore them; defensive teams can stifle them
upset_factors.notes: Dangerous first-round opponent — can score with anyone. But defensive issues limit ceiling.

teams.name: Iowa

kenpom.notes: Balanced profile — solid on both ends (32nd O, 28th D)

coach.name: Ben McCollum
coach.tournament_experience: First year at major D1 level — came from Northwest Missouri State (4 D2 titles)
coach.notes: 12 newcomers on 14-player roster. Preseason All-American Bennett Stirtz.

strengths: Balanced efficiency — solid on both ends
Bennett Stirtz is a proven scorer and leader
Big Ten schedule provides tournament preparation
KenPom rank (24th) better than NET (25th)
weaknesses: First-year head coach at D1 level — steep learning curve
12 newcomers out of 14 players — roster chemistry unknown
20-11 record — bubble team, not a lock (ESPN: 96% chance)
Lost to Nebraska 84-75 OT in regular season finale
teams.conference_tournament_status: No. 9 seed in Big Ten Tournament

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: New coach and roster may not handle tournament pressure well
upset_factors.notes: Classic 8/9 seed that could go either way. First-year coach at this level is a concern.

teams.name: Wisconsin

kenpom.notes: Strong offense (16th AdjO), average defense (53rd AdjD)

coach.name: Greg Gard
coach.tournament_experience: Multiple NCAA appearances, consistent Big Ten contender
coach.notes: Known for developing players and grinding out wins

key_players.name: John Blackwell
key_players.notes: Go-to scorer, especially in clutch moments

strengths: Strong offense — 83.3 PPG (36th nationally), AdjO rank 16
SRS: 19.17 (27th nationally) — quality wins
Multiple Top 10 road upsets — 2 among 5 most improbable wins of season
5th in Big Ten (14-6) — battle-tested
Strong SOS: 11.49 (20th nationally)
weaknesses: Average defense — 75.6 PPG allowed (230th), AdjD 53rd
9 losses — inconsistency at times
Lost to BYU 91-89 in 2025 NCAA tournament

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Can be outscored by elite offenses due to defensive gaps
upset_factors.notes: Classic Wisconsin — well-coached, plays above their talent level. Blackwell is a potential breakout March performer.

teams.name: Saint Louis

kenpom.notes: Balanced but not elite on either end. 40th KenPom despite 26-4 record — SOS concerns.

strengths: Dominant A-10 regular season — 26-4, 15-2 in conference
Ranked 25th in AP Poll
Strong record suggests team chemistry and consistency
weaknesses: Lost A-10 tournament quarterfinal to Loyola Chicago 64-72
Ended up in NIT, lost to Arkansas State 78-103 in first round
KenPom rank (40th) FAR below record — classic A-10 bubble team
Weak strength of schedule — wins not impressive to committee
NIT loss by 25 points raises serious questions about competitiveness vs NCAA-level teams
teams.conference_tournament_status: ELIMINATED — Lost A-10 QF, placed in NIT, lost R1

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Cannot compete with power conference teams (NIT blowout loss)
upset_factors.notes: WARNING: Did NOT make NCAA Tournament. NIT team only. 26-4 record was misleading — weak schedule inflated record. Remove from bracket consideration.

teams.name: Kentucky

coach.name: Mark Pope
coach.tournament_experience: Sweet 16 in Year 1 (2025). Former BYU coach.
coach.notes: Built roster primarily via transfer portal. Year 2 should be less chaotic.

key_players.name: Jayden Quaintance
key_players.notes: Transfer from Arizona State, projected top-5 pick in 2026 NBA Draft

key_players.name: Otega Oweh
key_players.notes: Transfer from Oklahoma, one of Pope's best players in Year 1

strengths: Elite talent — Quaintance (projected top-5 pick), Lowe (16.8/4.2/5.5)
Deep shooting — Williams, Jelavic, Johnson, Lowe, Chandler all lights-out
Sweet 16 experience from 2025
Mark Pope's roster continuity better in Year 2
Blue-blood program with championship DNA
weaknesses: 12 losses — most on this entire list
19-12 record underwhelming for Kentucky standards
Transfer-heavy roster still gelling
Lost to Tennessee 78-65 in 2025 Sweet Sixteen

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Inconsistency — capable of beating anyone or losing to anyone
upset_factors.three_point_variance: Multiple shooters but volume can be streaky
upset_factors.notes: Blue-blood deflator applies (0.88-0.92 multiplier per CLAUDE.md). Kentucky always dangerous in March but 12 losses is concerning. Quaintance is a game-changer if he's locked in.

teams.name: Utah State

kenpom.notes: Solid offense (25th AdjO), weak defense (63rd AdjD). Classic mid-major scorer.

coach.name: Jerrod Calhoun
coach.notes: Won outright Mountain West regular season title — 3rd all-time MW championship

strengths: Mountain West regular season champions (outright, 15-5)
12th 25-win season in program history
First time winning 25+ games in 4 straight seasons
Strong offense — 25th in AdjO
Consistent program — 4 straight NCAA appearances
weaknesses: Lost MW Tournament semifinal to Colorado State
Weak defense — 63rd in AdjD
KenPom rank (35th) well below NET (29th) — SOS concerns
Mid-major ceiling — may lack talent for deep run

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Defensive weakness can be exploited by high-powered offenses
upset_factors.notes: Consistent program but not a deep-run threat. 8/9 game is a coin flip. Conference tournament loss hurts momentum.

teams.name: Ohio State

kenpom.notes: Strong offense (18th AdjO), below-average defense (52nd AdjD)

coach.name: Jake Diebler
coach.tournament_experience: First NCAA tournament berth in 4 years if they make it
coach.notes: Entering pivotal second season with upperclassman-laden lineup

key_players.name: Bruce Thornton
key_players.stats: 25 pts, 7 ast in finale vs Indiana

strengths: Strong offense — 18th in AdjO nationally
Multiple scorers (Thornton, Mobley, Bynum all scored 18+ vs Indiana)
Beat Indiana 91-78 in regular season finale — strong finish
KenPom (26th) better than NET (30th)
Upperclassman-laden lineup — experience
weaknesses: 11 losses — inconsistent season
Defense below average — 52nd in AdjD
No NCAA tournament in 4 years — rust
8th in Big Ten — middle of the pack

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Defensive weaknesses can be exposed by elite offenses
upset_factors.notes: 7/10 matchup vs Kentucky is a classic coin-flip game. Both teams have similar profiles. Ohio State's offense could carry them.

teams.name: Georgia

kenpom.notes: Elite offense (12th AdjO), terrible defense (87th AdjD). Most offense-skewed profile on this list.

coach.name: Mike White
coach.tournament_experience: 2 consecutive NCAA tournament appearances (first time since 2001-02). Coach of the Year in 3 conferences (SEC, CUSA, WAC).
coach.notes: Ranked 11th in SEC pay but overperforming. Building historic program.

key_players.name: Jordan Ross
key_players.notes: Transfer from Saint Mary's — sure-fire impact player

strengths: Elite offense — 12th in AdjO nationally
22 wins is program record for regular season
Back-to-back NCAA appearances (first time in 24 years)
Mike White overperforming relative to resources
Four-guard lineups create matchup problems
Improved 3-pt shooting and assist-turnover ratio
weaknesses: Worst defense on this list — 87th in AdjD
Lost Asa Newell (projected first-rounder) and Silas Demary Jr. (to UConn)
Smaller, faster lineup may get bullied inside
SEC schedule doesn't forgive defensive lapses
notable_wins: Mississippi State (regular season finale — 22nd win, program record)

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: Any physical team with interior presence can exploit small lineup
upset_factors.notes: House money team — playing with nothing to lose. Program in historic territory. But 87th defense is alarming for tournament play.

teams.name: Miami FL

kenpom.notes: Well-balanced — 33rd O, 35th D. No glaring weakness.

coach.name: Jai Lucas
coach.tournament_experience: First year as head coach. Former assistant at Duke and Kentucky.
coach.notes: 17-win improvement over last season — largest turnaround in D1. 24 wins matches program record for regular season.

key_players.name: Malik Reneau
key_players.stats: 20.4 PPG (5th ACC), 6.8 RPG, 2.2 APG, 1 BPG, 56.8% FG, 35% 3PT

key_players.name: Tre Donaldson
key_players.stats: 23 pts in key game, 5 AST, 3 STL, surpassed 1000 career pts

key_players.name: Ernest Udeh Jr.
key_players.stats: 6.9 PPG, 9.9 RPG (3rd ACC), 1.1 STL, 1.1 BPG, 71.7% FG

strengths: Biggest turnaround in D1 — 17-win improvement
Balanced offense and defense — no glaring weakness
Strong interior — Udeh (9.9 RPG, 71.7% FG) + Reneau (20.4 PPG)
Won 7 of 8 in February — peaking at right time
13-4 in ACC — strong conference record
Multiple senior leaders (Reneau, Donaldson, Udeh)
weaknesses: First-year head coach in tournament — no HC postseason track record
KenPom (29th) lower than NET (32nd) inverted — may be fairly rated
ACC strength of schedule questions
No recent tournament pedigree for program

upset_factors.matchup_vulnerabilities: First-time HC in pressure situations is unknown variable
upset_factors.three_point_variance: Reneau at 35% from 3, Washington at 33.9% — decent but not elite
upset_factors.notes: Fascinating team — enormous turnaround, senior-laden, peaking in March. Jai Lucas has Duke/UK pedigree as assistant. Could be this year's Cinderella story or could flame out early.
